channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: GE Vernova’s CEO on thriving through tariffs and supply chain shifts
item.description: Amid tariff whiplash and the rejuggling of global trade, GE Vernova’s CEO Scott Strazik is finding a way to stay “relentlessly optimistic.
item.dc:creator: Robert Safian, Fast Company

item.title: Optus is hiking prices on its postpaid mobile plans, so I've found 3 cheap alternatives on the same network
item.description: Following a price hike for prepaid customers earlier this year, Optus has increased the cost and data limits for postpaid users.
item.dc:creator: zac.kelly@futurenet.com (Zachariah Kelly), TechRadar

item.title: TikTok starts testing Footnotes, a new feature that looks a lot like X’s Community Notes
item.description: TikTok is launching its own version of community notes on the platform, called “Footnotes.” The crowd-sourced approach to moderation, where users add additional context to posts, has become increasingly popular on social media networks after X (then called Twitter) first launched it in 2021.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: 5 things you need to know before using AI tools in performing legal due diligence in the world of government contracts
item.description: Recent advances in AI are revolutionizing legal due diligence by enhancing efficiency and accuracy. The post 5 things you need to know before using AI tools in performing legal due diligence in the world of government contracts first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg wraps up testimony in antitrust case
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg emphasized in federal court on Wednesday that he bought Instagram and WhatsApp because he saw value in the companies — not to take out competitors, as the Federal Trade Commission alleges in a historic antitrust trial.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Zeekr's new 007 GT looks like the ultimate family EV – with 10-minute charging and over 500 miles of range
item.description: Zeekr has launched a sleek, grand touring 'shooting brake' version of its 007 sedan available in China – and it should eventually go global, too.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Tech shares fall after Nvidia says new US controls on exports of AI chip will cost it $5.5 billion
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Shares in computer chipmakers slumped Wednesday after Nvidia said tighter U.S. government controls on exports of computer chips used for artificial intelligence will cost it an extra $5.5 billion.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hackers steal 1.6 million patient records in major healthcare breach
item.description: The CyberGuy explains why healthcare organizations are vulnerable to hackers after there have been a half dozen data breaches this year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nvidia says US chip tariffs mean it faces multi-billion dollar hit
item.description: Nvidia expects $5.5 billion in export-related costs for its H20 products alone in the first fiscal quarter.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Another California city bans ‘price-fixing’ software from RealPage to set rents
item.description: San Diego joins several other cities that have voted to ban RealPage software, saying it illegally boosts residential rent prices.
item.dc:creator: Phillip Molnar, The Mercury News

item.title: How today’s enterprise architect juggles strategy, tech and innovation
item.description: In today’s rapidly evolving business landscape, the role of the enterprise architect has become more crucial than ever, beyond the usual “bridge between business and IT.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: AI: the ultimate check against organizational well-being
item.description: There’s nothing like a technological paradigm shift to test your organization’s health and ability to absorb change. Just like businesses had to embrace mobility and cloud computing or risk being left behind, they’re now having to find ways to use AI effectively.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The Elder Scrolls 4: Oblivion Remaster Imminent - What You Need to Know
item.description: The long-rumored The Elder Scrolls 4: Oblivion remaster may be real, with potential images of the game appearing on developer Virtuos's website.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Tech shares fall after Nvidia says new US controls on exports of AI chip will cost it $5.5 billion
item.description: Shares in Nvidia and other tech companies slumped after the computer chipmaker said that tighter U.S. government controls on its exports of semiconductors used for artificial intelligence will cost it an extra $5.5 billion.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Cohere’s Embed 4 model helps enterprises search dynamic documents, ‘messy’ data
item.description: Embedding models help transform complex data — text, images, audio, and video — into numerical representations that computers can understand. The embeddings capture the semantic meaning of the data, making them useful for tasks like search, recommendation systems, and natural language processing.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Meta CEO Zuckerberg considered spinning off Instagram in 2018 over antitrust worries, email says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg once considered separating Instagram from its parent company due to worries about antitrust litigation, according to an email shown Tuesday on the second day of an antitrust trial alleging Meta illegally monopolized the social media market.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Hertz says hackers stole customer data in vendor breach
item.description: Hertz is notifying customers that hackers may have stolen personal information including credit card details and Social Security numbers during a data breach on one of its vendors.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: JRuby 10 brings faster startup times
item.description: JRuby 10, the latest release of the Ruby language variant built atop the JVM, has arrived, bringing startup time improvements, support for Java 21, and compatibility with Ruby 3.4. Release of JRuby 10 was announced April 14. JRuby 10 can be downloaded from jruby.org.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Accelerate Together
item.description: CrowdStrike’s field tech strategist explains how AWS Control Tower plus CrowdStrike Falcon lets new workloads inherit access controls in zero trust environments The post Accelerate Together — Zero Trust 2025: CrowdStrike’s Dennen Monks on ensuring none of your assets escape zero trust coverage first
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Nvidia Announces US-Based AI Supercomputer Production
item.description: Nvidia plans to produce AI supercomputers entirely in the US for the first time, commissioning over 1 million square feet of manufacturing space in Arizona and Texas.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Intel sells majority stake in Altera to Silver Lake investors in a deal valued at $8.75 billion
item.description: Intel announced Monday that it was selling the majority of its Altera chip business to technology investor Silver Lake in a deal valued at $8.75 billion.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Nvidia plans to manufacture AI chips in the US for the first time
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Nvidia announced Monday that it will produce its artificial intelligence supercomputers in the United States for the first time.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: As legacy of Ford fades in Brazil, China's BYD looks to extend global lead in EV sales
item.description: CAMACARI, Brazil (AP) — When Chinese automaker BYD Co. announced plans to build a massive factory in the hardscrabble city of Camacari, in Brazil’s northeastern Bahia state, locals saw a new beginning. After years of economic stagnation following Ford Motor Co.
item.dc:creator: FABIANO MAISONNAVE, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Sony hikes PlayStation 5 console prices for Europe, UK and Australia citing economic turmoil
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Sony said it will raise prices starting Monday for some PlayStation 5 video game consoles in Europe, Australia and New Zealand, citing global economic turmoil.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: DeepSeek’s open source movement
item.description: DeepSeek may have originated in China, but it stopped being Chinese the minute it was released on Hugging Face with an accompanying paper detailing its development.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: AI humanoid robot learns to mimic human emotions and behavior
item.description: A groundbreaking robot that's like a real-life Wall-E uses advanced artificial intelligence to replicate natural gestures and deliberate actions with striking accuracy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Japanese leader says he hopes Osaka expo will help reunite a divided world
item.description: OSAKA, Japan (AP) — Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba, speaking at Saturday's opening ceremony for the Osaka expo, expressed his hope that the event will help restore global unity in a world plagued by conflicts and trade wars.
item.dc:creator: AYAKA McGILL and MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Google Cloud Next 2025: News and insights
item.description: Google Cloud Next ’25, which runs from April 9-11 in Las Vegas is highlighting the latest advancements and future directions of Google Cloud Platform and cloud computing.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: OpenAI countersues Elon Musk in legal dispute over ChatGPT maker's business ambitions
item.description: OpenAI is suing Elon Musk for unfair competition and interfering with its business relationships with investors and customers, escalating a legal battle between the ChatGPT maker and the billionaire who helped bankroll the artificial intelligence startup a decade ago.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: One Tech Tip: Locking down your device when crossing borders
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Planning an international trip? Travelers should prepare for the possibility of extra scrutiny of their phones when crossing borders, especially when entering the United States. The Canadian government warned travelers in a recent travel advisory that U.S.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
